file.content:
# Changelog

## 1.2.0 (2026-03-18)

### Changed
- **Removed PreToolUse hook** — DB init moved to SessionStart (runs once, not per tool call)
- **PostToolUse matcher** set to `Skill` — only fires on Skill calls, not every tool
- SessionStart now handles lazy DB initialization (memory + intelligence) at session start
- SessionStart timeout increased to 10s (DB init + dispatcher rules + context)
- Hook registry reduced from 5 to 4 events — eliminates per-tool-call overhead
- Installer dynamically reads hook count from registry

### Fixed
- Clean up stale flat-format hook entries from v1.1.0 during update (migration fix)
- Clean up hooks for events removed from registry (e.g., PreToolUse) during update
- Remove function now handles both old flat format and new nested format

## 1.1.1 (2026-03-18)

### Fixed
- Hook registry format: use Claude Code's `matcher` + `hooks` array structure instead of flat format
- Hook merge/remove functions updated to handle nested hook group structure
- Tests updated to validate correct hooks API format

## 1.1.0 (2026-03-18)

### Added
- **Runtime hooks**: 5 Claude Code lifecycle hooks (SessionStart, PreToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, PostToolUse, Stop)
- **SessionStart hook**: Injects dispatcher rules, business context, and active intelligence patterns at session start
- **PreToolUse hook**: Lazy initialization of Memory and Intelligence engine databases on first tool use
- **UserPromptSubmit hook**: Preflight dependency checks for `/founder-os:*` commands
- **PostToolUse hook**: Observation logging to intelligence database for founder-os skill calls
- **Stop hook**: Session cleanup, temp file removal, and session summary logging
- **Shared SQLite helper** (`db-helper.mjs`): Zero-dependency database operations via sqlite3 CLI
- **Hook registry** (`hook-registry.json`): Centralized hook configuration for all 5 events
- **Evals pipeline**: Shipped eval-runner, checks, and rubrics in distribution package
- **Hook merge/remove functions** in `settings-json.js` for idempotent hook management

### Changed
- Installer now registers runtime hooks (Phase 2a) and verifies DB initialization (Phase 2b)
- Updater merges hook registry on update
- Uninstaller removes founderOS hook entries while preserving user hooks
- Dispatcher preflight checks moved from subagent to UserPromptSubmit hook

## 1.0.0

- Initial release
- `--init` command for fresh install and updates
- `--uninstall` command with backup restoration
- `--status` and `--version` commands
- CLAUDE.md marker-based merge
- settings.json deep merge
- Manifest-based checksum tracking for safe updates

file.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

Founder OS is a single-plugin AI automation ecosystem for SMB founders, built on Claude Code using the official Anthropic plugin format. 33 namespaces cover email triage, meeting prep, report generation, CRM sync, and more. Each namespace = working tool + blog post + welcome gift component.

**Notion is the source of truth** for all task specs. Local `docs/specs/` files are synced copies. When in doubt, fetch from Notion database `[NaluForge] Projects & Tasks` (project: "🚀 Founder OS: 30 Plugin Development").

**Documentation**: All docs live under `docs/` — see `docs/INDEX.md` for full map:
- `docs/getting-started/` — Setup guide, FAQ, troubleshooting
- `docs/specs/` — Plugin specifications (per-pillar) + research docs (evals, Notion CLI migration)
- `docs/plans/` — Completed architecture decisions and design docs
- `docs/superpowers/` — Future development specs (8) and implementation plans (8)
- `docs/agent-specs/` — Agent deployment specs for complex plugins (P03, P20)
- `docs/reference/` — Condensed references (AIOS concepts, UAT summary)
- `docs/reports/` — Audit reports

## Architecture

### Plugin Architecture

The repository is a single Claude Code plugin. The `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` manifest at the repo root enables automatic discovery. Commands, skills, and agents are organized by namespace:

```
.claude-plugin/plugin.json                     # Single plugin manifest
commands/<namespace>/<action>.md               # → /founder-os:<namespace>:<action>
skills/<namespace>/<skill-name>/SKILL.md       # Domain knowledge per namespace
agents/<namespace>/config.json + <agent>.md    # Agent teams (7 namespaces)
```

There are 33 command namespaces (one per logical tool area) and 7 agent team namespaces (`briefing`, `client`, `inbox`, `invoice`, `prep`, `report`, `sow`).

### Social Content

Blog posts and welcome gifts live in a separate directory from the plugin structure:
```
social/
└── [plugin-short-name]/       # e.g., inbox-zero, follow-up-tracker
    ├── blog/
    │   └── [slug].md          # Week N blog post
    └── welcome-gift/
        └── [name].md          # User cheat sheet or template
```

### Four Pillars

| Pillar | Emoji | Plugins | Focus |
|--------|-------|---------|-------|

# Open-Source Repository Preparation — Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (if subagents available) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Transform founder-workspace into a clean, well-documented open-source repository by excluding internal tooling, restructuring docs, and adding standard community files.

**Architecture:** Gitignore-based exclusion of internal directories (.beads, .claude, .claude-flow, .swarm) with `git rm --cached` to untrack 294 files. Docs restructured by renaming current `docs/` to `internal_docs/` (gitignored) and promoting `docs/site/` content to a flattened root `docs/`. New root README, LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING, and GitHub templates added.

**Tech Stack:** Git, Markdown, YAML (GitHub issue templates)

**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/[26]-2026-03-19-open-source-repo-design.md`

---

## Chunk 1: Gitignore & Internal File Removal

### Task 1: Replace .gitignore with full directory exclusions

**Files:**
- Modify: `.gitignore` (complete replacement)

- [ ] **Step 1: Replace `.gitignore` contents**

Write the new `.gitignore` that replaces all partial rules with full directory exclusions:

```gitignore
# Internal development tooling
.beads/
.claude/
.claude-flow/
.swarm/
.entire/
.worktrees/

# Internal docs (development specs, plans, reports)
internal_docs/

# Dependencies
node_modules/

# OS files
.DS_Store

# Release artifacts (built tarballs, not the dist/ directory itself)
dist/*.tgz

# Process files (NOT package lockfiles — those should be committed)
*.pid
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify .gitignore is correct**

Run: `cat .gitignore`
Expected: New content with full directory exclusions, no partial file rules remaining.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit .gitignore**

```bash
git add .gitignore
git commit -m "chore: replace .gitignore with full directory exclusions for open-source prep"
```

---

### Task 2: Remove internal directories from git tracking

**Files:**
- Untrack: `.beads/` (19 files), `.claude/` (265 files), `.claude-flow/` (7 files), `.swarm/` (3 files)

- [ ] **Step 1: Remove .beads/ from tracking**

```bash
git rm -r --cached .beads/
```

Expected: `rm '.beads/.gitignore'`, `rm '.beads/.migration-hint-ts'`, etc. (19 files)

- [ ] **Step 2: Remove .claude/ from tracking**

```bash
git rm -r --cached .claude/
```

Expected: ~265 files removed from index. Files remain on disk.

- [ ] **Step 3: Remove .claude-flow/ from tracking**

```bash
git rm -r --cached .claude-flow/
```

Expected: ~7 files removed from index.

- [ ] **Step 4: Remove .swarm/ from tracking**

```bash
git rm -r --cached .swarm/
```

Expected: ~3 files removed from index.

- [ ] **Step 5: Verify files are untracked but still on disk**

```bash
git status | head -20   # should show deleted files (from index only)
ls .beads/ .claude/ .claude-flow/ .swarm/  # should all still exist
```

- [ ] **Step 6: Commit untracking**

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: remove internal tooling directories from git tracking

Files remain on disk for local development but are excluded from the
public repository via .gitignore."
```

---

## Chunk 2: Docs Restructuring

### Task 3: Rename docs/ to internal_docs/

**Files:**
- Move: `docs/` → `internal_docs/`

- [ ] **Step 1: Move docs to internal_docs using git mv**

```bash
git mv docs internal_docs
```

Expected: All files under `docs/` moved to `internal_docs/`. Git tracks the rename.

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify the move**

```bash
ls internal_docs/
```

Expected: `agent-specs`, `founder-os-development-timeline.md`, `getting-started`, `INDEX.md`, `plans`, `reference`, `reports`, `site`, `specs`, `superpowers`

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit the rename**

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: rename docs/ to internal_docs/ for open-source prep

Internal documentation (specs, plans, reports, agent-specs) moves to
internal_docs/ which is gitignored. Public docs will be promoted from
the site/ subdirectory in the next step."
```

---

### Task 4: Create new public docs/ from site content

**Files:**
- Create: `docs/` (new directory)
- Source: `internal_docs/site/docs/*` and `internal_docs/site/landing/`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create new docs directory and flatten site content**

```bash
mkdir -p docs
cp -r internal_docs/site/docs/* docs/
cp -r internal_docs/site/landing docs/landing/
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify the new structure**

```bash
ls docs/
```

Expected: `agents`, `commands`, `deep-dives`, `extending`, `index.md`, `installation.md`, `landing`

```bash
ls docs/landing/
```

Expected: `about.md`, `getting-started.md`, `how-it-works.md`, `index.md`, `use-cases.md`

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit the new public docs**

```bash
git add docs/
git commit -m "docs: promote site documentation to root docs/ directory

Flattened docs/site/docs/ and docs/site/landing/ into a single docs/
directory for public-facing documentation."
```

---

### Task 5: Fix internal links in new docs/

**Files:**
- Modify: `docs/index.md` (3 link fixes)
- Modify: `docs/installation.md` (1 link fix)
- Modify: `docs/landing/use-cases.md` (~22 link fixes)
- Modify: `docs/landing/how-it-works.md` (1 link fix)
- Modify: `docs/landing/index.md` (1 link fix)
- Modify: `docs/deep-dives/hooks-system.md` (1 link fix)
- Modify: `docs/deep-dives/dispatcher.md` (1 link fix)

- [ ] **Step 1: Find all broken links**

```bash
grep -rn '\.\./landing/' docs/ --include='*.md'
grep -rn '\.\./docs/' docs/ --include='*.md'
grep -rn '\.\./\.\./landing/' docs/ --include='*.md'
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Fix links from docs/*.md referencing ../landing/**

In `docs/index.md`, replace all occurrences of `../landing/` with `./landing/`:
- `../landing/getting-started.md` → `./landing/getting-started.md` (3 occurrences)

In `docs/installation.md`, replace:
- `../landing/getting-started.md` → `./landing/getting-started.md` (1 occurrence)

- [ ] **Step 3: Fix links from docs/landing/*.md referencing ../docs/**

In `docs/landing/use-cases.md`, replace all occurrences of `../docs/commands.md` with `../commands/index.md`:
- There are ~22 occurrences of `](../docs/commands.md)` → `](../commands/index.md)`

Also fix the final link:
- `../docs/commands.md` → `../commands/index.md`

In `docs/landing/how-it-works.md`, replace:
- `../docs/commands/index.md` → `../commands/index.md`

In `docs/landing/index.md`, replace:
- `../docs/getting-started.md` → `./getting-started.md`

- [ ] **Step 4: Fix links from docs/deep-dives/*.md referencing ../../landing/**

In `docs/deep-dives/hooks-system.md`, replace:
- `../../landing/getting-started.md` → `../landing/getting-started.md`

In `docs/deep-dives/dispatcher.md`, replace:
- `../../landing/getting-started.md` → `../landing/getting-started.md`

- [ ] **Step 5: Verify no broken links remain**

```bash
grep -rn '\.\./landing/' docs/ --include='*.md'   # should only show docs/deep-dives refs (now ../landing/)
grep -rn '\.\./docs/' docs/ --include='*.md'       # should be empty
grep -rn '\.\./\.\./landing/' docs/ --include='*.md'  # should be empty
```

- [ ] **Step 6: Commit link fixes**

```bash
git add docs/
git commit -m "docs: fix internal links after docs restructuring"
```

---

## Chunk 3: CLAUDE.md & Root Files

### Task 6: Preserve and rewrite CLAUDE.md

**Files:**
- Copy: `CLAUDE.md` → `.claude/CLAUDE-internal.md`
- Modify: `CLAUDE.md` (rewrite for public contributors)

- [ ] **Step 1: Copy current CLAUDE.md to .claude/ for preservation**

```bash
cp CLAUDE.md .claude/CLAUDE-internal.md
```

This file is already gitignored (`.claude/` is in `.gitignore`), so it stays local only.

- [ ] **Step 2: Rewrite root CLAUDE.md for public contributors**

Replace the entire content of `CLAUDE.md` with:

```markdown
# Founder OS

AI-powered business automation plugin for Claude Code. Turns Claude Code into a command center for email triage, meeting prep, report generation, CRM sync, and 30+ other founder workflows.

## Repository Structure

```
founder-workspace/
  ├── development/         # Source code
  │   ├── commands/        # Slash commands (/founder-os:<namespace>:<action>)
  │   ├── skills/          # Domain knowledge per namespace
  │   ├── agents/          # Agent team definitions
  │   ├── scripts/         # Build and utility scripts
  │   ├── _infrastructure/ # Infrastructure helpers (preflight, hooks, etc.)
  │   └── install.sh       # Plugin installer
  ├── dist/                # Distribution package (npm)
  └── docs/                # Public documentation
      ├── commands/        # Command reference (35 namespaces)
      ├── agents/          # Agent team documentation
      ├── deep-dives/      # Architecture internals
      ├── extending/       # Customization guides
      └── landing/         # Product landing page content
```

## Adding a New Command

Commands live in `development/commands/<namespace>/<action>.md`. Each command is a markdown file that Claude Code loads as a slash command.

1. Create the command file: `development/commands/<namespace>/<action>.md`
2. Add the corresponding skill (if needed): `development/skills/<namespace>/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`
3. Register any new dependencies in `development/_infrastructure/preflight/dependency-registry.json`

## Adding a New Skill

Skills live in `development/skills/<namespace>/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`. Skills provide domain knowledge that commands reference.

## Adding a New Agent Team

Agent teams live in `development/agents/<namespace>/`. Each team has a `config.json` and one or more agent markdown files.

## Build & Test

```bash
# Run distribution tests
cd dist && npm test

# Package for release
cd dist && npm pack
```

## Contributing

See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for development setup, PR process, and coding standards.
```

```markdown
# Contributing to Founder OS

Thank you for your interest in contributing to Founder OS! This guide will help you get started.

## Development Setup

1. **Clone the repository:**

   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/thecloudtips/founder-workspace.git
   cd founder-workspace
   ```

2. **Install Claude Code** if you haven't already — Founder OS is a Claude Code plugin.

3. **Install the plugin locally:**

   ```bash
   cd development
   ./install.sh
   ```

4. **Verify the installation:**

   ```bash
   /founder-os:setup:verify
   ```

## How to Contribute

### Reporting Bugs

- Use the [Bug Report](https://github.com/thecloudtips/founder-workspace/issues/new?template=bug_report.yml) issue template
- Include your environment details (OS, Node version, Claude Code version)
- Include steps to reproduce the issue

### Suggesting Features

- Use the [Feature Request](https://github.com/thecloudtips/founder-workspace/issues/new?template=feature_request.yml) issue template
- Describe the problem you're trying to solve
- Explain your proposed solution

### Submitting Changes

1. Fork the repository
2. Create a feature branch: `git checkout -b feat/my-feature`
3. Make your changes following the existing code patterns
4. Test your changes
5. Commit with a descriptive message: `git commit -m "feat: add my feature"`
6. Push to your fork: `git push origin feat/my-feature`
7. Open a Pull Request

### PR Requirements

- Clear description of what changed and why
- Reference any related issues
- No breaking changes without discussion first
- Follow existing code style and patterns

## Code Style

- Follow the patterns established in existing commands, skills, and agents
- Commands go in `development/commands/<namespace>/<action>.md`
- Skills go in `development/skills/<namespace>/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`
- Agents go in `development/agents/<namespace>/`

## Code of Conduct

This project follows the [Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md). By participating, you are expected to uphold this code.

## Questions?

- Visit [fos.naluforge.com](https://fos.naluforge.com) for product documentation
- Read the [docs/](docs/) for technical documentation
- Open a [Discussion](https://github.com/thecloudtips/founder-workspace/discussions) for questions
```

```markdown
# Security Policy

## Supported Versions

| Version | Supported          |
| ------- | ------------------ |
| 1.2.x   | Yes                |
| 1.1.x   | Security fixes only|
| < 1.1   | No                 |

## Reporting a Vulnerability

**Please do not report security vulnerabilities through public GitHub issues.**

Instead, please report them via email to **security@founderos.dev**.

You should receive a response within 48 hours. If for some reason you do not, please follow up via email to ensure we received your original message.

Please include:

- Type of issue (e.g., command injection, path traversal, data exposure)
- Full paths of source file(s) related to the issue
- Step-by-step instructions to reproduce the issue
- Impact of the issue and how an attacker might exploit it

## Scope

The following are in scope:

- Command injection through skill/command parameters
- Path traversal in file operations
- Credential or API key exposure
- Unauthorized access to connected services (Notion, Gmail, etc.)

The following are out of scope:

- Issues in Claude Code itself (report to Anthropic)
- Issues in third-party MCP servers
- Social engineering attacks
```

  │   ├── agents/          # Agent team definitions
  │   ├── scripts/         # Build and utility scripts
  │   ├── _infrastructure/ # Infrastructure helpers (preflight, hooks, etc.)
  │   └── install.sh       # Plugin installer
  ├── dist/                # Distribution package (npm)
  └── docs/                # Public documentation
      ├── commands/        # Command reference (35 namespaces)
      ├── agents/          # Agent team documentation
      ├── deep-dives/      # Architecture internals
      ├── extending/       # Customization guides
      └── landing/         # Product landing page content
```

## Adding a New Command

Commands live in `development/commands/<namespace>/<action>.md`. Each command is a markdown file that Claude Code loads as a slash command.

1. Create the command file: `development/commands/<namespace>/<action>.md`
2. Add the corresponding skill (if needed): `development/skills/<namespace>/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`
3. Register any new dependencies in `development/_infrastructure/preflight/dependency-registry.json`

## Adding a New Skill

Skills live in `development/skills/<namespace>/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`. Skills provide domain knowledge that commands reference.

## Adding a New Agent Team

Agent teams live in `development/agents/<namespace>/`. Each team has a `config.json` and one or more agent markdown files.

## Build & Test

```bash
# Run distribution tests
cd dist && npm test

# Package for release
cd dist && npm pack
```

## Contributing

See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for development setup, PR process, and coding standards.
```

```markdown
# Contributing to Founder OS

Thank you for your interest in contributing to Founder OS! This guide will help you get started.

## Development Setup

1. **Clone the repository:**

   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/thecloudtips/founder-workspace.git
   cd founder-workspace
   ```

2. **Install Claude Code** if you haven't already — Founder OS is a Claude Code plugin.

3. **Install the plugin locally:**

   ```bash
   cd development
   ./install.sh
   ```

4. **Verify the installation:**

   ```bash
   /founder-os:setup:verify
   ```

## How to Contribute

### Reporting Bugs

- Use the [Bug Report](https://github.com/thecloudtips/founder-workspace/issues/new?template=bug_report.yml) issue template
- Include your environment details (OS, Node version, Claude Code version)
- Include steps to reproduce the issue

### Suggesting Features

- Use the [Feature Request](https://github.com/thecloudtips/founder-workspace/issues/new?template=feature_request.yml) issue template
- Describe the problem you're trying to solve
- Explain your proposed solution

### Submitting Changes

1. Fork the repository
2. Create a feature branch: `git checkout -b feat/my-feature`
3. Make your changes following the existing code patterns
4. Test your changes
5. Commit with a descriptive message: `git commit -m "feat: add my feature"`
6. Push to your fork: `git push origin feat/my-feature`
7. Open a Pull Request

### PR Requirements

- Clear description of what changed and why
- Reference any related issues
- No breaking changes without discussion first
- Follow existing code style and patterns

## Code Style

- Follow the patterns established in existing commands, skills, and agents
- Commands go in `development/commands/<namespace>/<action>.md`
- Skills go in `development/skills/<namespace>/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`
- Agents go in `development/agents/<namespace>/`

## Code of Conduct

This project follows the [Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md). By participating, you are expected to uphold this code.

## Questions?

- Visit [fos.naluforge.com](https://fos.naluforge.com) for product documentation
- Read the [docs/](docs/) for technical documentation
- Open a [Discussion](https://github.com/thecloudtips/founder-workspace/discussions) for questions
```

```markdown
# Security Policy

## Supported Versions

| Version | Supported          |
| ------- | ------------------ |
| 1.2.x   | Yes                |
| 1.1.x   | Security fixes only|
| < 1.1   | No                 |

## Reporting a Vulnerability

**Please do not report security vulnerabilities through public GitHub issues.**

Instead, please report them via email to **security@founderos.dev**.

You should receive a response within 48 hours. If for some reason you do not, please follow up via email to ensure we received your original message.

Please include:

- Type of issue (e.g., command injection, path traversal, data exposure)
- Full paths of source file(s) related to the issue
- Step-by-step instructions to reproduce the issue
- Impact of the issue and how an attacker might exploit it

## Scope

The following are in scope:

- Command injection through skill/command parameters
- Path traversal in file operations
- Credential or API key exposure
- Unauthorized access to connected services (Notion, Gmail, etc.)

The following are out of scope:

- Issues in Claude Code itself (report to Anthropic)
- Issues in third-party MCP servers
- Social engineering attacks
```

   284→# Founder OS
   285→
   286→AI-powered business automation plugin for Claude Code. Turns Claude Code into a command center for email triage, meeting prep, report generation, CRM sync, and 30+ other founder workflows.
   287→
   288→## Repository Structure
   289→
   290→```
   291→founder-workspace/
   292→  ├── development/         # Source code
   293→  │   ├── commands/        # Slash commands (/founder-os:<namespace>:<action>)
   294→  │   ├── skills/          # Domain knowledge per namespace
   295→  │   ├── agents/          # Agent team definitions
   296→  │   ├── scripts/         # Build and utility scripts
   297→  │   ├── _infrastructure/ # Infrastructure helpers (preflight, hooks, etc.)
   298→  │   └── install.sh       # Plugin installer
   299→  ├── dist/                # Distribution package (npm)
   300→  └── docs/                # Public documentation
   301→      ├── commands/        # Command reference (35 namespaces)
   302→      ├── agents/          # Agent team documentation
   303→      ├── deep-dives/      # Architecture internals
   304→      ├── extending/       # Customization guides
   305→      └── landing/         # Product landing page content
   306→```
   307→
   308→## Adding a New Command
   309→
   310→Commands live in `development/commands/<namespace>/<action>.md`. Each command is a markdown file that Claude Code loads as a slash command.
   311→
   312→1. Create the command file: `development/commands/<namespace>/<action>.md`
   313→2. Add the corresponding skill (if needed): `development/skills/<namespace>/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`
   314→3. Register any new dependencies in `development/_infrastructure/preflight/dependency-registry.json`
   315→
   316→## Adding a New Skill
   317→
   318→Skills live in `development/skills/<namespace>/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`. Skills provide domain knowledge that commands reference.
   319→
   320→## Adding a New Agent Team
   321→
   322→Agent teams live in `development/agents/<namespace>/`. Each team has a `config.json` and one or more agent markdown files.
   323→
   324→## Build & Test
   325→
   326→```bash
   327→# Run distribution tests
   328→cd dist && npm test
   329→
   330→# Package for release
   331→cd dist && npm pack
   332→```
   333→
   334→## Contributing
   335→
   336→See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for development setup, PR process, and coding standards.
   337→```

   397→
   398→```markdown
   399→# Contributing to Founder OS
   400→
   401→Thank you for your interest in contributing to Founder OS! This guide will help you get started.
   402→
   403→## Development Setup
   404→
   405→1. **Clone the repository:**
   406→
   407→   ```bash
   408→   git clone https://github.com/thecloudtips/founder-workspace.git
   409→   cd founder-workspace
   410→   ```
   411→
   412→2. **Install Claude Code** if you haven't already — Founder OS is a Claude Code plugin.
   413→
   414→3. **Install the plugin locally:**
   415→
   416→   ```bash
   417→   cd development
   418→   ./install.sh
   419→   ```
   420→
   421→4. **Verify the installation:**
   422→
   423→   ```bash
   424→   /founder-os:setup:verify
   425→   ```
   426→
   427→## How to Contribute
   428→
   429→### Reporting Bugs
   430→
   431→- Use the [Bug Report](https://github.com/thecloudtips/founder-workspace/issues/new?template=bug_report.yml) issue template
   432→- Include your environment details (OS, Node version, Claude Code version)
   433→- Include steps to reproduce the issue
   434→
   435→### Suggesting Features
   436→
   437→- Use the [Feature Request](https://github.com/thecloudtips/founder-workspace/issues/new?template=feature_request.yml) issue template
   438→- Describe the problem you're trying to solve
   439→- Explain your proposed solution
   440→
   441→### Submitting Changes
   442→
   443→1. Fork the repository
   444→2. Create a feature branch: `git checkout -b feat/my-feature`
   445→3. Make your changes following the existing code patterns
   446→4. Test your changes
   447→5. Commit with a descriptive message: `git commit -m "feat: add my feature"`
   448→6. Push to your fork: `git push origin feat/my-feature`
   449→7. Open a Pull Request
   450→
   451→### PR Requirements
   452→
   453→- Clear description of what changed and why
   454→- Reference any related issues
   455→- No breaking changes without discussion first
   456→- Follow existing code style and patterns
   457→
   458→## Code Style
   459→
   460→- Follow the patterns established in existing commands, skills, and agents
   461→- Commands go in `development/commands/<namespace>/<action>.md`
   462→- Skills go in `development/skills/<namespace>/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`
   463→- Agents go in `development/agents/<namespace>/`
   464→
   465→## Code of Conduct
   466→
   467→This project follows the [Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md). By participating, you are expected to uphold this code.
   468→
   469→## Questions?
   470→
   471→- Visit [fos.naluforge.com](https://fos.naluforge.com) for product documentation
   472→- Read the [docs/](docs/) for technical documentation
   473→- Open a [Discussion](https://github.com/thecloudtips/founder-workspace/discussions) for questions
   474→```
   475→

   481→```

   513→### Task 10: Create SECURITY.md
   514→
   515→**Files:**
   516→- Create: `SECURITY.md`

   518→- [ ] **Step 1: Write SECURITY.md**
   519→
   520→```markdown
   521→# Security Policy
   522→
   523→## Supported Versions
   524→
   525→| Version | Supported          |
   526→| ------- | ------------------ |
   527→| 1.2.x   | Yes                |
   528→| 1.1.x   | Security fixes only|
   529→| < 1.1   | No                 |
   530→
   531→## Reporting a Vulnerability
   532→
   533→**Please do not report security vulnerabilities through public GitHub issues.**
   534→
   535→Instead, please report them via email to **security@founderos.dev**.
   536→
   537→You should receive a response within 48 hours. If for some reason you do not, please follow up via email to ensure we received your original message.
   538→
   539→Please include:
   540→
   541→- Type of issue (e.g., command injection, path traversal, data exposure)
   542→- Full paths of source file(s) related to the issue
   543→- Step-by-step instructions to reproduce the issue
   544→- Impact of the issue and how an attacker might exploit it
   545→
   546→## Scope
   547→
   548→The following are in scope:
   549→
   550→- Command injection through skill/command parameters
   551→- Path traversal in file operations
   552→- Credential or API key exposure
   553→- Unauthorized access to connected services (Notion, Gmail, etc.)
   554→
   555→The following are out of scope:
   556→
   557→- Issues in Claude Code itself (report to Anthropic)
   558→- Issues in third-party MCP servers
   559→- Social engineering attacks
   560→```
   561→

   567→```

1→# [26] Open-Source Repository Preparation Design
     2→
     3→**Date:** 2026-03-19
     4→**Status:** Approved
     5→**Approach:** A — Clean Gitignore + Restructure
     6→
     7→## Problem
     8→
     9→The founder-workspace repository contains internal development tooling (.beads, .claude, .claude-flow, .swarm, .entire) mixed with the public product (development/, dist/). The docs/ directory mixes internal specs/plans with public-facing documentation. There is no root README or standard open-source files (LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING, CODE_OF_CONDUCT, SECURITY). The repo is not presentable as a public open-source project.
    10→
    11→## Goal
    12→
    13→Transform the repository into a clean, well-documented open-source project that:
    14→- Excludes all internal development tooling from git tracking
    15→- Presents comprehensive public documentation
    16→- Has a compelling GitHub landing page (README)
    17→- Includes all standard open-source community files
    18→- Maintains internal files on disk for local development
    19→
    20→## Design
    21→
    22→### 1. Gitignore Rewrite & Internal File Removal
    23→
    24→The current `.gitignore` has partial rules for internal directories (individual files within `.beads/`, `.claude-flow/`, `.swarm/`). These partial rules will be **replaced** with full directory exclusions. The new `.gitignore` supersedes all existing content.
    25→
    26→**New `.gitignore` (complete replacement):**
    27→
    28→```gitignore
    29→# Internal development tooling
    30→.beads/
    31→.claude/
    32→.claude-flow/
    33→.swarm/
    34→.entire/
    35→.worktrees/
    36→
    37→# Internal docs (development specs, plans, reports)
    38→internal_docs/
    39→
    40→# Dependencies
    41→node_modules/
    42→
    43→# OS files
    44→.DS_Store
    45→
    46→# Release artifacts (built tarballs, not the dist/ directory itself)
    47→dist/*.tgz
    48→
    49→# Claude Code local settings (machine-specific)
    50→.claude/settings.local.json
    51→
    52→# Process files (but NOT package lockfiles — those should be committed)
    53→*.pid
    54→```
    55→
    56→**Note on lockfiles:** `package-lock.json` and similar lockfiles are NOT ignored. They ensure reproducible installs for contributors. Only process PID files are ignored.
    57→
    58→**Note on `.claude/settings.json`:** While `.claude/` is fully excluded, the public project configuration is stored in `development/CLAUDE.md` and the root `CLAUDE.md`. Contributors don't need `.claude/settings.json` — it contains internal workflow definitions (265 agents, helpers, commands) used for development orchestration.
    59→
    60→**Remove from git tracking (files stay on disk):**
    61→
    62→| Directory | Tracked Files | Action |
    63→|-----------|--------------|--------|
    64→| `.beads/` | 19 | `git rm -r --cached` |
    65→| `.claude/` | 265 | `git rm -r --cached` |
    66→| `.claude-flow/` | 7 | `git rm -r --cached` |
    67→| `.swarm/` | 3 | `git rm -r --cached` |
    68→| **Total** | **294** | Removed from tracking only |
    69→
    70→### 2. Docs Restructuring
    71→
    72→**Actual disk layout of `docs/site/`:**
    73→
    74→```
    75→docs/site/
    76→  ├── docs/                  # documentation content (nested under site/)
    77→  │   ├── index.md
    78→  │   ├── installation.md
    79→  │   ├── agents/
    80→  │   ├── commands/
    81→  │   ├── deep-dives/
    82→  │   └── extending/
    83→  └── landing/               # landing page content
    84→      ├── index.md
    85→      ├── about.md
    86→      ├── getting-started.md
    87→      ├── how-it-works.md
    88→      └── use-cases.md
    89→```
    90→
    91→**Step 1: Rename `docs/` to `internal_docs/`**
    92→
    93→Move the entire current docs directory to `internal_docs/`. This directory is gitignored and stays on disk for local development.
    94→
    95→**Step 2: Flatten and promote `internal_docs/site/` to root `docs/`**
    96→
    97→The `internal_docs/site/` directory has a two-level structure (`site/docs/` and `site/landing/`). We **flatten** this by merging both directories into the new root `docs/`:
    98→
    99→```bash
   100→# Create new docs from site content
   101→cp -r internal_docs/site/docs/* docs/      # copies index.md, installation.md, agents/, commands/, etc.
   102→cp -r internal_docs/site/landing docs/landing/  # copies landing/ as a subdirectory
   103→```
   104→
   105→**Target structure after flattening:**
   106→
   107→```
   108→docs/
   109→  ├── index.md              # Documentation home (from site/docs/)
   110→  ├── installation.md       # Full setup guide (from site/docs/)
   111→  ├── agents/               # 10 agent team docs (from site/docs/)
   112→  │   ├── index.md
   113→  │   ├── briefing-team.md
   114→  │   ├── client-team.md
   115→  │   └── ... (11 files)
   116→  ├── commands/             # 35 namespace command reference (from site/docs/)
   117→  │   ├── index.md
   118→  │   ├── actions.md
   119→  │   ├── briefing.md
   120→  │   └── ... (36 files)
   121→  ├── deep-dives/           # Architecture internals (from site/docs/)
   122→  │   ├── dispatcher.md
   123→  │   ├── evals-framework.md
   124→  │   ├── hooks-system.md
   125→  │   ├── intelligence-engine.md
   126→  │   └── memory-engine.md
   127→  ├── extending/            # Customization guides (from site/docs/)
   128→  │   ├── custom-namespaces.md
   129→  │   ├── scout.md
   130→  │   └── workflows.md
   131→  └── landing/              # Landing page content (from site/landing/)
   132→      ├── index.md
   133→      ├── about.md
   134→      ├── getting-started.md
   135→      ├── how-it-works.md
   136→      └── use-cases.md
   137→```
   138→
   139→**Step 3: Fix internal links**
   140→
   141→After flattening, the relative paths between docs and landing content change:
   142→
   143→| File location | Old reference | New reference |
   144→|--------------|---------------|---------------|
   145→| `docs/index.md` | `../landing/getting-started.md` | `./landing/getting-started.md` |
   146→| `docs/landing/*.md` | `../docs/commands/index.md` | `../commands/index.md` |
   147→| `docs/landing/*.md` | `../docs/agents/index.md` | `../agents/index.md` |
   148→| `docs/landing/*.md` | `../docs/installation.md` | `../installation.md` |
   149→
   150→A grep for `../landing/` and `../docs/` across all files in the new `docs/` directory will identify all links to fix.
   151→
   152→### 3. Root README.md (GitHub Landing Page)
   153→
   154→A comprehensive README serving as the first thing visitors see:
   155→
   156→**Structure:**
   157→
   158→1. **Hero Section**
   159→   - Project name with tagline: "AI Chief of Staff for Founders"
   160→   - One-sentence description
   161→   - Install command badge
   162→   - Links to fos.naluforge.com, blog.naluforge.com, now.naluforge.com
   163→
   164→2. **What is Founder OS?**
   165→   - 2-3 paragraph overview
   166→   - Drawn from fos.naluforge.com messaging
   167→   - Emphasis: open-source, free, no signup, Claude Code plugin
   168→
   169→3. **Quick Start**
   170→   - 3 steps: install, configure, run first command
   171→   - Code blocks with actual commands
   172→
   173→4. **Four Pillars Overview**
   174→   - Daily Work (inbox, meetings, briefings)
   175→   - Code Without Coding (reports, invoices, proposals)
   176→   - Integrations (Gmail, Drive, Notion, Slack)
   177→   - Meta & Growth (ROI tracking, workflows, learning)
   178→   - Each with 2-3 example commands
   179→
   180→5. **By the Numbers**
   181→   - Table: 35 namespaces, 120+ commands, 10 agent teams, 6 integrations
   182→
   183→6. **Feature Highlights**
   184→   - Memory Engine, Intelligence Engine, Agent Teams, Scout, Workflows
   185→   - Brief description of each (2-3 sentences)
   186→
   187→7. **Documentation Links**
   188→   - Getting Started Guide
   189→   - Command Reference
   190→   - Agent Teams
   191→   - Deep Dives
   192→   - Extending FOS
   193→
   194→8. **Links**
   195→   - fos.naluforge.com — Product site
   196→   - blog.naluforge.com — Blog & tutorials
   197→   - now.naluforge.com — NaluForge (parent company)
   198→
   199→9. **Contributing**
   200→   - Link to CONTRIBUTING.md
   201→   - Brief welcome message
   202→
   203→10. **License**
   204→    - MIT with copyright line
   205→
   206→### 4. Open-Source Standard Files
   207→
   208→#### LICENSE (root)
   209→
   210→MIT License, copied from `dist/LICENSE`. Copyright line: "Copyright (c) 2026 NaluForge / Founder OS"
   211→
   212→#### CONTRIBUTING.md
   213→
   214→Sections:
   215→- Welcome & project overview
   216→- Development setup (clone, install deps, run tests)
   217→- How to contribute (fork → branch → PR)
   218→- PR requirements (description, tests, no breaking changes)
   219→- Code style (follow existing patterns)
   220→- Reporting bugs (link to issue template)
   221→- Suggesting features (link to feature request template)
   222→- Code of Conduct reference
   223→
   224→#### CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
   225→
   226→Contributor Covenant v2.1 (industry standard, used by most major open-source projects).
   227→
   228→#### SECURITY.md
   229→
   230→- Supported versions table
   231→- How to report vulnerabilities (email, not public issues)
   232→- Expected response timeline
   233→- Scope (what qualifies as a security issue)
   234→
   235→#### CHANGELOG.md (root)
   236→
   237→Copied from `dist/CHANGELOG.md`, maintained at root going forward. The `dist/CHANGELOG.md` is left as-is for now; the build/release process can be updated separately to source from root. This is noted as a known tech debt item.
   238→
   239→#### .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.yml
   240→
   241→YAML-based GitHub issue template (not markdown frontmatter) for better UI rendering:
   242→- Description textarea (required)
   243→- Steps to reproduce textarea (required)
   244→- Expected vs actual behavior textareas
   245→- Environment dropdown (OS, Node version)
   246→- FOS version input (required)
   247→
   248→#### .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/feature_request.yml
   249→
   250→YAML-based template:
   251→- Problem description textarea (required)
   252→- Proposed solution textarea
   253→- Alternatives considered textarea
   254→- Use case textarea (required)
   255→
   256→#### .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml
   257→
   258→Template chooser configuration with links to docs and discussions.
   259→
   260→#### .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md
   261→
   262→Checklist (markdown is fine for PR templates):
   263→- Description of changes
   264→- Related issue number
   265→- Type (bugfix, feature, docs, refactor)
   266→- Testing done
   267→- Breaking changes?
   268→
   269→#### .github/FUNDING.yml
   270→
   271→Sponsorship configuration linking to NaluForge:
   272→- Custom URL to now.naluforge.com or relevant sponsorship page
   273→
   274→### 5. CLAUDE.md Rewrite
   275→
   276→**Current root CLAUDE.md** references beads, swarm, claude-flow, hive-mind — all internal tooling. It needs rewriting for public contributors.
   277→
   278→**Action:**
   279→1. Copy current `CLAUDE.md` to `.claude/CLAUDE-internal.md` (already gitignored via `.claude/` rule, stays on disk)
   280→2. Rewrite root `CLAUDE.md` for public contributors:
   281→
   282→**New CLAUDE.md structure:**
   283→- Project overview (what Founder OS is)
   284→- Repository structure (development/, dist/, docs/)
   285→- How to add a new command
   286→- How to add a new skill
   287→- How to add a new agent
   288→- Build & test commands
   289→- Link to CONTRIBUTING.md
   290→- No references to internal tooling (beads, swarm, claude-flow, hive-mind)
   291→
   292→## Execution Order
   293→
   294→1. Update `.gitignore` — **replace** entire file with new content (supersedes partial rules)
   295→2. `git rm -r --cached` for .beads/, .claude/, .claude-flow/, .swarm/
   296→3. Rename `docs/` → `internal_docs/` (git mv)
   297→4. Create new `docs/` directory
   298→5. Flatten `internal_docs/site/docs/*` → `docs/` and `internal_docs/site/landing/` → `docs/landing/`
   299→6. Fix internal links in new `docs/` files (grep for `../landing/`, `../docs/`)
   300→7. Copy current CLAUDE.md → `.claude/CLAUDE-internal.md`
   301→8. Create root files: README.md, LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, SECURITY.md, CHANGELOG.md
   302→9. Create .github/ templates (bug_report.yml, feature_request.yml, config.yml, PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md, FUNDING.yml)
   303→10. Rewrite root CLAUDE.md for public contributors
   304→11. Verify no internal references leak into public files
   305→12. Commit everything in a single "chore: prepare repository for open-source" commit
   306→
   307→## Known Tech Debt
   308→
   309→- `dist/CHANGELOG.md` duplicates root `CHANGELOG.md` — build process should be updated to source from root
   310→- CI/CD pipelines not included (future work)
   311→- Branch protection rules not configured
   312→- NPM publishing automation not set up
   313→
   314→## Out of Scope
   315→
   316→- CI/CD pipelines
   317→- Automated release workflows
   318→- GitHub Actions
   319→- Branch protection rules
   320→- NPM publishing automation
   321→- Repository transfer or rename
   322→

Now I have everything I need. Let me analyze the findings carefully.

**Reviewing:** Implementation plan `/Users/lhalicki/coding_projects/founder-workspace/docs/superpowers/plans/[25]-2026-03-19-open-source-repo-preparation.md` against design spec `/Users/lhalicki/coding_projects/founder-workspace/docs/superpowers/specs/[26]-2026-03-19-open-source-repo-design.md`.

---

## Review Findings

### CRITICAL

---

**Issue 1: Task 6 (CLAUDE.md backup) will silently fail — `.claude/` has already been untracked in Task 2**

Confidence: 95

File: Plan, Task 6, Step 1 (line 271)

After Task 2 removes `.claude/` from git tracking and the new `.gitignore` excludes `.claude/`, the `cp CLAUDE.md .claude/CLAUDE-internal.md` command at Task 6 Step 1 will still work on disk (the directory exists locally). However, the plan's comment says "This file is already gitignored (.claude/ is in .gitignore), so it stays local only" — but at the time Task 6 runs, `.claude/` may not exist on disk if an engineer executes this on a freshly cloned repo (which will be the case for every contributor). This is misleading framing but the deeper issue is that the preservation step provides zero protection for anyone other than the current developer. For the original developer running this sequentially, it will work — but the plan should acknowledge this is local-only with no fallback.

More critically: Task 6 is listed in Chunk 3 but the spec's **Execution Order (step 7)** places the CLAUDE.md copy before creating root files (step 8). The plan matches this ordering, so no sequence bug here — but see Issue 4 below for a related ordering problem.

No fix required for this specific concern; it is an acceptable local-only preservation.

---

**Issue 2: Task 5 link fixes are incomplete — `docs/landing/index.md` link is not to `../docs/getting-started.md`, it's to `../docs/getting-started.md` (a non-existent file), and the plan's fix is wrong**

Confidence: 97

File: Plan, Task 5, Step 3 (lines 235-236); actual source: `/Users/lhalicki/coding_projects/founder-workspace/docs/site/landing/index.md` line 210

The actual link in `docs/site/landing/index.md` is:
```
../docs/getting-started.md
```

The plan says to fix it to `./getting-started.md`. But after flattening, the `getting-started.md` file lives at `docs/landing/getting-started.md` — it is **inside** landing/, not at the `docs/` root. The correct fix should be:
- `../docs/getting-started.md` → `./getting-started.md`

This is actually correct as written (`./getting-started.md` from inside `docs/landing/` resolves to `docs/landing/getting-started.md`). No bug here — just confirming the plan is right.

Withdraw — this is correct.

---

**Issue 3: `use-cases.md` link pattern is wrong — the plan tries to fix `../docs/commands.md` but the actual file reference is `../docs/commands.md` (no `index.md`) and there is no `commands.md` at the docs root after restructuring**

Confidence: 98

File: Plan, Task 5, Step 3 (lines 226-227); actual source: `/Users/lhalicki/coding_projects/founder-workspace/docs/site/landing/use-cases.md`

The grep results confirm the actual links in `use-cases.md` are `../docs/commands.md` — linking to a flat file `commands.md`, not `../docs/commands/index.md`.

After restructuring, the correct path from `docs/landing/use-cases.md` to the commands index will be `../commands/index.md`.

The plan's fix — `](../docs/commands.md)` → `](../commands/index.md)` — is **correct**. Confirmed.

However, the plan misses the **last line of `use-cases.md` (line 275)**:

```
Or explore the [full command reference](../docs/commands.md) to see everything FOS can do.
```

The plan only describes fixing the table occurrences (~22) but the final sentence at line 275 also contains `../docs/commands.md`. The plan does acknowledge this ("Also fix the final link"), so this is covered. Confirmed correct.

---

**Issue 4: `docs/landing/how-it-works.md` fix is WRONG — the actual link target differs from what the plan states**

Confidence: 97

File: Plan, Task 5, Step 3 (lines 231-232); actual source confirmed via grep

The actual link in `docs/site/landing/how-it-works.md` (line 251) is:
```
../docs/commands/index.md
```

The plan says to fix: `../docs/commands/index.md` → `../commands/index.md`

After restructuring, this file is at `docs/landing/how-it-works.md`. The correct new path to the commands index is `../commands/index.md`.

The plan's fix is **correct**. Confirmed.

---

**Issue 5 (CRITICAL): `docs/landing/index.md` link target is `../docs/getting-started.md` — there is no `getting-started.md` at the docs root after flattening, and the plan's fix resolves to the wrong file**

Confidence: 95

File: Plan, Task 5, Step 3 (line 235-236)

The actual link at `docs/site/landing/index.md` line 210 is `../docs/getting-started.md`. After restructuring, `docs/landing/index.md` lives at `docs/landing/index.md`. The fix `../docs/getting-started.md` → `./getting-started.md` resolves to `docs/landing/getting-started.md`, which is correct — `getting-started.md` is a landing file. This is correct.

Withdraw — confirmed correct again.

---

**Issue 6 (CRITICAL): Task 2, Step 6 uses `git add -A` after `git rm --cached` — this will re-add the untracked files back to the index**

Confidence: 92

File: Plan, Task 2, Step 6 (lines 111-117)

After running `git rm -r --cached` on `.beads/`, `.claude/`, `.claude-flow/`, `.swarm/`, those files are removed from the index. The `.gitignore` was committed in Task 1, so those directories are now ignored. Running `git add -A` at this point should be safe because `git add -A` respects `.gitignore` — ignored files are not re-added. This is correct behavior.

Withdraw — `git add -A` is safe here because the `.gitignore` commit happened first.

---

**Issue 7 (CRITICAL): The `.gitignore` in the plan is missing a line present in the spec**

Confidence: 100

File: Plan, Task 1, Step 1 (lines 26-49) vs. Spec section 1 (lines 28-54)

The spec's `.gitignore` includes this line that the plan's version omits:

```
# Claude Code local settings (machine-specific)
.claude/settings.local.json
```

The spec explicitly includes `.claude/settings.local.json` as a standalone rule (in addition to the full `.claude/` exclusion). While `.claude/` already covers this file, the spec includes the rule with its own comment as belt-and-suspenders documentation. The plan silently drops this entry.

Severity: Minor — functionally redundant since `.claude/` covers it, but it represents a deviation from the spec that an engineer would not catch without side-by-side comparison.

Suggested fix: Add the following two lines to the plan's `.gitignore` after the `dist/*.tgz` block:
```
# Claude Code local settings (machine-specific)
.claude/settings.local.json
```

---

**Issue 8 (CRITICAL ORDER DEPENDENCY): Task 3 `git mv docs internal_docs` will fail if `internal_docs/` already exists**

Confidence: 85

File: Plan, Task 3, Step 1 (line 131)

`git mv docs internal_docs` will fail with "destination already exists" if any prior operation or untracked state created an `internal_docs/` directory. Since `.gitignore` now includes `internal_docs/`, if an engineer had previously run a partial attempt and `internal_docs/` exists on disk as an untracked directory, the `git mv` will error. The plan has no guard step.

Suggested fix: Add a preflight check before Step 1:
```bash
[ -d internal_docs ] && echo "ERROR: internal_docs/ already exists — remove it before proceeding" && exit 1
```

---

**Issue 9 (MAJOR): Task 4 uses `cp -r` not `git mv` — the new `docs/` files will be untracked, not tracked**

Confidence: 88

File: Plan, Task 4, Step 1 (lines 163-168); Spec section 2

The plan uses `cp -r internal_docs/site/docs/* docs/` to populate the new public `docs/`. Since `internal_docs/` is gitignored (it was moved there in Task 3), the source files are no longer tracked. The destination `docs/` directory is new and untracked. The plan then commits with `git add docs/` which will correctly stage all the new files.

This is functionally correct — there is no way to use `git mv` from a gitignored directory. However, the plan does not mention that `internal_docs/` is gitignored at this point, which means git will not show the source files in `git status`. An engineer might be confused when `ls internal_docs/site/` shows files but `git status` does not.

More importantly: if `docs/site/` contains any files that were never committed (e.g., the files were always gitignored or untracked), the `cp -r` will copy them into the new tracked `docs/`. Looking at the git status output at the start of the conversation, no `docs/site/` untracked files appear listed, so this is likely safe.

No fix required — the `git add docs/` in Step 3 handles this correctly.

---

**Issue 10 (MAJOR): Spec execution order step 12 ("Commit everything in a single commit") conflicts with the plan's per-task commits**

Confidence: 90

File: Spec, Execution Order, step 12 (line 305) vs. Plan throughout

The spec says:
> "Commit everything in a single 'chore: prepare repository for open-source' commit"

But the plan uses granular per-task commits (15 separate commits across all chunks). This is a direct contradiction. The plan never addresses or justifies this deviation from the spec.

An engineer following the plan will produce 15 commits. An engineer following the spec will produce 1. These are irreconcilable without a decision.

Suggested fix: The plan should either (a) explicitly note that it deviates from the spec's single-commit approach and justify the granular commits as preferable for rollback/reviewability, or (b) replace all intermediate commits with `git add` staging steps and add a single final commit at the end of Task 15.

---

**Issue 11 (MAJOR): `docs/index.md` has 2 occurrences of `../landing/getting-started.md`, not 3**

Confidence: 88

File: Plan, Task 5, Step 2 (line 219)

The plan states there are 3 occurrences of `../landing/getting-started.md` in `docs/index.md`. The grep results show exactly 2 occurrences in `docs/site/docs/index.md` (lines 17 and 58). The plan overcounts by one. If an engineer is checking their work against "expected: 3 replacements" they will think something failed.

Suggested fix: Change the annotation from "(3 occurrences)" to "(2 occurrences)".

---

**Issue 12 (MINOR): Task 9 (CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) has no concrete content — an engineer cannot execute it without external lookup**

Confidence: 85

File: Plan, Task 9, Step 1 (lines 492-503)

Every other task in the plan provides complete, copy-paste-ready file contents. Task 9 only says "Use the Contributor Covenant v2.1 text" and lists section names. An engineer must look up the full text externally. This violates the plan's own "actionability" standard — all other tasks have complete inline content.

Severity: Minor relative to other issues, but it is the only task that requires external lookup to execute.

Suggested fix: Either include the full Contributor Covenant v2.1 text inline (it is ~150 lines and public domain), or add a direct URL: `https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/`

---

### Summary

| # | Severity | Issue |
|---|----------|-------|
| 7 | Minor | `.gitignore` in plan is missing one line from the spec (`# Claude Code local settings` + `.claude/settings.local.json`) |
| 8 | Major | No guard against pre-existing `internal_docs/` before `git mv docs internal_docs` |
| 10 | Major | Plan uses 15 granular commits; spec mandates a single final commit — unaddressed contradiction |
| 11 | Major | `docs/index.md` link count is 2 occurrences, plan states 3 — engineer verification will fail |
| 12 | Minor | Task 9 (CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) is not actionable — no inline content, requires external lookup |

All other checks (git commands, `cp -r` ordering, link path math for all 7 affected files, `dist/CHANGELOG.md` and `dist/LICENSE` existence, `.gitignore` rule ordering) verified correct against the actual file tree.

against the design spec at /Users/lhalicki/coding_projects/founder-workspace/docs/superpowers/specs/[26]-2026-03-19-open-source-repo-design.md

Check for:

1. **Completeness** — Does every item in the spec's execution order have a corresponding task? Are any steps missing?
2. **Correctness** — Are git commands correct? Will the file operations work in the order specified? Are the link fixes accurate based on the actual file paths?
3. **Actionability** — Can an engineer execute each step without ambiguity? Are file paths exact? Is code complete (not "add validation")?
4. **Order dependencies** — Will any task fail if executed in the listed order? Are there hidden dependencies?
5. **Risk** — Could any step accidentally delete files, break the working tree, or lose data?

Focus on HIGH-CONFIDENCE issues only. Return structured findings with severity (critical/major/minor) and suggested fixes.

The actual link at `docs/site/landing/index.md` line 210 is `../docs/getting-started.md`. After restructuring, `docs/landing/index.md` lives at `docs/landing/index.md`. The fix `../docs/getting-started.md` → `./getting-started.md` resolves to `docs/landing/getting-started.md`, which is correct — `getting-started.md` is a landing file. This is correct.

Withdraw — confirmed correct again.

---

**Issue 6 (CRITICAL): Task 2, Step 6 uses `git add -A` after `git rm --cached` — this will re-add the untracked files back to the index**

Confidence: 92

File: Plan, Task 2, Step 6 (lines 111-117)

After running `git rm -r --cached` on `.beads/`, `.claude/`, `.claude-flow/`, `.swarm/`, those files are removed from the index. The `.gitignore` was committed in Task 1, so those directories are now ignored. Running `git add -A` at this point should be safe because `git add -A` respects `.gitignore` — ignored files are not re-added. This is correct behavior.

Withdraw — `git add -A` is safe here because the `.gitignore` commit happened first.

---

**Issue 7 (CRITICAL): The `.gitignore` in the plan is missing a line present in the spec**

Confidence: 100

File: Plan, Task 1, Step 1 (lines 26-49) vs. Spec section 1 (lines 28-54)

The spec's `.gitignore` includes this line that the plan's version omits:

```
# Claude Code local settings (machine-specific)
.claude/settings.local.json
```

The spec explicitly includes `.claude/settings.local.json` as a standalone rule (in addition to the full `.claude/` exclusion). While `.claude/` already covers this file, the spec includes the rule with its own comment as belt-and-suspenders documentation. The plan silently drops this entry.

Severity: Minor — functionally redundant since `.claude/` covers it, but it represents a deviation from the spec that an engineer would not catch without side-by-side comparison.

Suggested fix: Add the following two lines to the plan's `.gitignore` after the `dist/*.tgz` block:
```
# Claude Code local settings (machine-specific)
.claude/settings.local.json
```

---

**Issue 8 (CRITICAL ORDER DEPENDENCY): Task 3 `git mv docs internal_docs` will fail if `internal_docs/` already exists**

Confidence: 85

File: Plan, Task 3, Step 1 (line 131)

`git mv docs internal_docs` will fail with "destination already exists" if any prior operation or untracked state created an `internal_docs/` directory. Since `.gitignore` now includes `internal_docs/`, if an engineer had previously run a partial attempt and `internal_docs/` exists on disk as an untracked directory, the `git mv` will error. The plan has no guard step.

Suggested fix: Add a preflight check before Step 1:
```bash
[ -d internal_docs ] && echo "ERROR: internal_docs/ already exists — remove it before proceeding" && exit 1
```

---

**Issue 9 (MAJOR): Task 4 uses `cp -r` not `git mv` — the new `docs/` files will be untracked, not tracked**

Confidence: 88

File: Plan, Task 4, Step 1 (lines 163-168); Spec section 2

The plan uses `cp -r internal_docs/site/docs/* docs/` to populate the new public `docs/`. Since `internal_docs/` is gitignored (it was moved there in Task 3), the source files are no longer tracked. The destination `docs/` directory is new and untracked. The plan then commits with `git add docs/` which will correctly stage all the new files.

This is functionally correct — there is no way to use `git mv` from a gitignored directory. However, the plan does not mention that `internal_docs/` is gitignored at this point, which means git will not show the source files in `git status`. An engineer might be confused when `ls internal_docs/site/` shows files but `git status` does not.

More importantly: if `docs/site/` contains any files that were never committed (e.g., the files were always gitignored or untracked), the `cp -r` will copy them into the new tracked `docs/`. Looking at the git status output at the start of the conversation, no `docs/site/` untracked files appear listed, so this is likely safe.

No fix required — the `git add docs/` in Step 3 handles this correctly.

---

**Issue 10 (MAJOR): Spec execution order step 12 ("Commit everything in a single commit") conflicts with the plan's per-task commits**

Confidence: 90

File: Spec, Execution Order, step 12 (line 305) vs. Plan throughout

The spec says:
> "Commit everything in a single 'chore: prepare repository for open-source' commit"

But the plan uses granular per-task commits (15 separate commits across all chunks). This is a direct contradiction. The plan never addresses or justifies this deviation from the spec.

An engineer following the plan will produce 15 commits. An engineer following the spec will produce 1. These are irreconcilable without a decision.

Suggested fix: The plan should either (a) explicitly note that it deviates from the spec's single-commit approach and justify the granular commits as preferable for rollback/reviewability, or (b) replace all intermediate commits with `git add` staging steps and add a single final commit at the end of Task 15.

---

**Issue 11 (MAJOR): `docs/index.md` has 2 occurrences of `../landing/getting-started.md`, not 3**

Confidence: 88

File: Plan, Task 5, Step 2 (line 219)

The plan states there are 3 occurrences of `../landing/getting-started.md` in `docs/index.md`. The grep results show exactly 2 occurrences in `docs/site/docs/index.md` (lines 17 and 58). The plan overcounts by one. If an engineer is checking their work against "expected: 3 replacements" they will think something failed.

Suggested fix: Change the annotation from "(3 occurrences)" to "(2 occurrences)".

---

**Issue 12 (MINOR): Task 9 (CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) has no concrete content — an engineer cannot execute it without external lookup**

Confidence: 85

File: Plan, Task 9, Step 1 (lines 492-503)

Every other task in the plan provides complete, copy-paste-ready file contents. Task 9 only says "Use the Contributor Covenant v2.1 text" and lists section names. An engineer must look up the full text externally. This violates the plan's own "actionability" standard — all other tasks have complete inline content.

Severity: Minor relative to other issues, but it is the only task that requires external lookup to execute.

Suggested fix: Either include the full Contributor Covenant v2.1 text inline (it is ~150 lines and public domain), or add a direct URL: `https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/`

---

### Summary

| # | Severity | Issue |
|---|----------|-------|
| 7 | Minor | `.gitignore` in plan is missing one line from the spec (`# Claude Code local settings` + `.claude/settings.local.json`) |
| 8 | Major | No guard against pre-existing `internal_docs/` before `git mv docs internal_docs` |
| 10 | Major | Plan uses 15 granular commits; spec mandates a single final commit — unaddressed contradiction |
| 11 | Major | `docs/index.md` link count is 2 occurrences, plan states 3 — engineer verification will fail |
| 12 | Minor | Task 9 (CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) is not actionable — no inline content, requires external lookup |

All other checks (git commands, `cp -r` ordering, link path math for all 7 affected files, `dist/CHANGELOG.md` and `dist/LICENSE` existence, `.gitignore` rule ordering) verified correct against the actual file tree.

Good review. Let me fix the issues found:

1. **Issue 10 (single vs multiple commits):** I'll keep granular commits — better for rollback and review. Adding a note.
2. **Issue 11 (link count):** Fix 3 → 2 occurrences.
3. **Issue 8 (guard check):** Add preflight check before `git mv`.
4. **Issue 12 (CODE_OF_CONDUCT):** Add the URL for the full text.
5. **Issue 7 (.gitignore line):** Add the redundant but spec-matching line.

   401→Thank you for your interest in contributing to Founder OS! This guide will help you get started.

   403→## Development Setup

   405→1. **Clone the repository:**

   427→## How to Contribute
   428→
   429→### Reporting Bugs
   430→
   431→- Use the [Bug Report](https://github.com/thecloudtips/founder-workspace/issues/new?template=bug_report.yml) issue template
   432→- Include your environment details (OS, Node version, Claude Code version)
   433→- Include steps to reproduce the issue
   434→
   435→### Suggesting Features
   436→

   438→- Describe the problem you're trying to solve
   439→- Explain your proposed solution
   440→
   441→### Submitting Changes
   442→
   443→1. Fork the repository
   444→2. Create a feature branch: `git checkout -b feat/my-feature`
   445→3. Make your changes following the existing code patterns
   446→4. Test your changes
   447→5. Commit with a descriptive message: `git commit -m "feat: add my feature"`
   448→6. Push to your fork: `git push origin feat/my-feature`
   449→7. Open a Pull Request

   451→### PR Requirements

   453→- Clear description of what changed and why
   454→- Reference any related issues
   455→- No breaking changes without discussion first
   456→- Follow existing code style and patterns

   458→## Code Style

   520→```markdown
   521→# Security Policy
   522→
   523→## Supported Versions
   524→
   525→| Version | Supported          |
   526→| ------- | ------------------ |
   527→| 1.2.x   | Yes                |
   528→| 1.1.x   | Security fixes only|
   529→| < 1.1   | No                 |
   530→
   531→## Reporting a Vulnerability
   532→
   533→**Please do not report security vulnerabilities through public GitHub issues.**
   534→
   535→Instead, please report them via email to **security@founderos.dev**.
   536→
   537→You should receive a response within 48 hours. If for some reason you do not, please follow up via email to ensure we received your original message.
   538→
   539→Please include:
   540→
   541→- Type of issue (e.g., command injection, path traversal, data exposure)
   542→- Full paths of source file(s) related to the issue
   543→- Step-by-step instructions to reproduce the issue
   544→- Impact of the issue and how an attacker might exploit it
   545→
   546→## Scope
   547→
   548→The following are in scope:
   549→
   550→- Command injection through skill/command parameters
   551→- Path traversal in file operations
   552→- Credential or API key exposure
   553→- Unauthorized access to connected services (Notion, Gmail, etc.)
   554→
   555→The following are out of scope:
   556→
   557→- Issues in Claude Code itself (report to Anthropic)
   558→- Issues in third-party MCP servers
   559→- Social engineering attacks
   560→```

# Open-Source Repository Preparation — Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (if subagents available) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Transform founder-workspace into a clean, well-documented open-source repository by excluding internal tooling, restructuring docs, and adding standard community files.

**Architecture:** Gitignore-based exclusion of internal directories (.beads, .claude, .claude-flow, .swarm) with `git rm --cached` to untrack 294 files. Docs restructured by renaming current `docs/` to `internal_docs/` (gitignored) and promoting `docs/site/` content to a flattened root `docs/`. New root README, LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING, and GitHub templates added.

**Tech Stack:** Git, Markdown, YAML (GitHub issue templates)

**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/[26]-2026-03-19-open-source-repo-design.md`

---

## Chunk 1: Gitignore & Internal File Removal

### Task 1: Replace .gitignore with full directory exclusions

**Files:**
- Modify: `.gitignore` (complete replacement)

- [ ] **Step 1: Replace `.gitignore` contents**

Write the new `.gitignore` that replaces all partial rules with full directory exclusions:

```gitignore
# Internal development tooling
.beads/
.claude/
.claude-flow/
.swarm/
.entire/
.worktrees/

# Internal docs (development specs, plans, reports)
internal_docs/

# Dependencies
node_modules/

# OS files
.DS_Store

# Release artifacts (built tarballs, not the dist/ directory itself)
dist/*.tgz

# Process files (NOT package lockfiles — those should be committed)
*.pid
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify .gitignore is correct**

Run: `cat .gitignore`
Expected: New content with full directory exclusions, no partial file rules remaining.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit .gitignore**

```bash
git add .gitignore
git commit -m "chore: replace .gitignore with full directory exclusions for open-source prep"
```

---

### Task 2: Remove internal directories from git tracking

**Files:**
- Untrack: `.beads/` (19 files), `.claude/` (265 files), `.claude-flow/` (7 files), `.swarm/` (3 files)

- [ ] **Step 1: Remove .beads/ from tracking**

```bash
git rm -r --cached .beads/
```

Expected: `rm '.beads/.gitignore'`, `rm '.beads/.migration-hint-ts'`, etc. (19 files)

- [ ] **Step 2: Remove .claude/ from tracking**

```bash
git rm -r --cached .claude/
```

Expected: ~265 files removed from index. Files remain on disk.

- [ ] **Step 3: Remove .claude-flow/ from tracking**

```bash
git rm -r --cached .claude-flow/
```

Expected: ~7 files removed from index.

- [ ] **Step 4: Remove .swarm/ from tracking**

```bash
git rm -r --cached .swarm/
```

Expected: ~3 files removed from index.

- [ ] **Step 5: Verify files are untracked but still on disk**

```bash
git status | head -20   # should show deleted files (from index only)
ls .beads/ .claude/ .claude-flow/ .swarm/  # should all still exist
```

- [ ] **Step 6: Commit untracking**

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: remove internal tooling directories from git tracking

Files remain on disk for local development but are excluded from the
public repository via .gitignore."
```

---

## Chunk 2: Docs Restructuring

### Task 3: Rename docs/ to internal_docs/

**Files:**
- Move: `docs/` → `internal_docs/`

- [ ] **Step 1: Move docs to internal_docs using git mv**

```bash
git mv docs internal_docs
```

Expected: All files under `docs/` moved to `internal_docs/`. Git tracks the rename.

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify the move**

```bash
ls internal_docs/
```

Expected: `agent-specs`, `founder-os-development-timeline.md`, `getting-started`, `INDEX.md`, `plans`, `reference`, `reports`, `site`, `specs`, `superpowers`

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit the rename**

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: rename docs/ to internal_docs/ for open-source prep

Internal documentation (specs, plans, reports, agent-specs) moves to
internal_docs/ which is gitignored. Public docs will be promoted from
the site/ subdirectory in the next step."
```

---

### Task 4: Create new public docs/ from site content

**Files:**
- Create: `docs/` (new directory)
- Source: `internal_docs/site/docs/*` and `internal_docs/site/landing/`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create new docs directory and flatten site content**

```bash
mkdir -p docs
cp -r internal_docs/site/docs/* docs/
cp -r internal_docs/site/landing docs/landing/
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify the new structure**

```bash
ls docs/
```

Expected: `agents`, `commands`, `deep-dives`, `extending`, `index.md`, `installation.md`, `landing`

```bash
ls docs/landing/
```

Expected: `about.md`, `getting-started.md`, `how-it-works.md`, `index.md`, `use-cases.md`

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit the new public docs**

```bash
git add docs/
git commit -m "docs: promote site documentation to root docs/ directory

Flattened docs/site/docs/ and docs/site/landing/ into a single docs/
directory for public-facing documentation."
```

---

### Task 5: Fix internal links in new docs/

**Files:**
- Modify: `docs/index.md` (3 link fixes)
- Modify: `docs/installation.md` (1 link fix)
- Modify: `docs/landing/use-cases.md` (~22 link fixes)
- Modify: `docs/landing/how-it-works.md` (1 link fix)
- Modify: `docs/landing/index.md` (1 link fix)
- Modify: `docs/deep-dives/hooks-system.md` (1 link fix)
- Modify: `docs/deep-dives/dispatcher.md` (1 link fix)

- [ ] **Step 1: Find all broken links**

```bash
grep -rn '\.\./landing/' docs/ --include='*.md'
grep -rn '\.\./docs/' docs/ --include='*.md'
grep -rn '\.\./\.\./landing/' docs/ --include='*.md'
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Fix links from docs/*.md referencing ../landing/**

In `docs/index.md`, replace all occurrences of `../landing/` with `./landing/`:
- `../landing/getting-started.md` → `./landing/getting-started.md` (3 occurrences)

In `docs/installation.md`, replace:
- `../landing/getting-started.md` → `./landing/getting-started.md` (1 occurrence)

- [ ] **Step 3: Fix links from docs/landing/*.md referencing ../docs/**

In `docs/landing/use-cases.md`, replace all occurrences of `../docs/commands.md` with `../commands/index.md`:
- There are ~22 occurrences of `](../docs/commands.md)` → `](../commands/index.md)`

Also fix the final link:
- `../docs/commands.md` → `../commands/index.md`

In `docs/landing/how-it-works.md`, replace:
- `../docs/commands/index.md` → `../commands/index.md`

In `docs/landing/index.md`, replace:
- `../docs/getting-started.md` → `./getting-started.md`

- [ ] **Step 4: Fix links from docs/deep-dives/*.md referencing ../../landing/**

In `docs/deep-dives/hooks-system.md`, replace:
- `../../landing/getting-started.md` → `../landing/getting-started.md`

In `docs/deep-dives/dispatcher.md`, replace:
- `../../landing/getting-started.md` → `../landing/getting-started.md`

- [ ] **Step 5: Verify no broken links remain**

```bash
grep -rn '\.\./landing/' docs/ --include='*.md'   # should only show docs/deep-dives refs (now ../landing/)
grep -rn '\.\./docs/' docs/ --include='*.md'       # should be empty
grep -rn '\.\./\.\./landing/' docs/ --include='*.md'  # should be empty
```

- [ ] **Step 6: Commit link fixes**

```bash
git add docs/
git commit -m "docs: fix internal links after docs restructuring"
```

---

## Chunk 3: CLAUDE.md & Root Files

### Task 6: Preserve and rewrite CLAUDE.md

**Files:**
- Copy: `CLAUDE.md` → `.claude/CLAUDE-internal.md`
- Modify: `CLAUDE.md` (rewrite for public contributors)

- [ ] **Step 1: Copy current CLAUDE.md to .claude/ for preservation**

```bash
cp CLAUDE.md .claude/CLAUDE-internal.md
```

This file is already gitignored (`.claude/` is in `.gitignore`), so it stays local only.

- [ ] **Step 2: Rewrite root CLAUDE.md for public contributors**

Replace the entire content of `CLAUDE.md` with:

```markdown
# Founder OS

AI-powered business automation plugin for Claude Code. Turns Claude Code into a command center for email triage, meeting prep, report generation, CRM sync, and 30+ other founder workflows.

## Repository Structure

```
founder-workspace/
  ├── development/         # Source code
  │   ├── commands/        # Slash commands (/founder-os:<namespace>:<action>)
  │   ├── skills/          # Domain knowledge per namespace
  │   ├── agents/          # Agent team definitions
  │   ├── scripts/         # Build and utility scripts
  │   ├── _infrastructure/ # Infrastructure helpers (preflight, hooks, etc.)
  │   └── install.sh       # Plugin installer
  ├── dist/                # Distribution package (npm)
  └── docs/                # Public documentation
      ├── commands/        # Command reference (35 namespaces)
      ├── agents/          # Agent team documentation
      ├── deep-dives/      # Architecture internals
      ├── extending/       # Customization guides
      └── landing/         # Product landing page content
```

## Adding a New Command

Commands live in `development/commands/<namespace>/<action>.md`. Each command is a markdown file that Claude Code loads as a slash command.

1. Create the command file: `development/commands/<namespace>/<action>.md`
2. Add the corresponding skill (if needed): `development/skills/<namespace>/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`
3. Register any new dependencies in `development/_infrastructure/preflight/dependency-registry.json`

## Adding a New Skill

Skills live in `development/skills/<namespace>/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`. Skills provide domain knowledge that commands reference.

## Adding a New Agent Team

Agent teams live in `development/agents/<namespace>/`. Each team has a `config.json` and one or more agent markdown files.

## Build & Test

```bash
# Run distribution tests
cd dist && npm test

# Package for release
cd dist && npm pack
```

## Contributing

See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for development setup, PR process, and coding standards.
```

```markdown
# Contributing to Founder OS

Thank you for your interest in contributing to Founder OS! This guide will help you get started.

## Development Setup

1. **Clone the repository:**

   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/thecloudtips/founder-workspace.git
   cd founder-workspace
   ```

2. **Install Claude Code** if you haven't already — Founder OS is a Claude Code plugin.

3. **Install the plugin locally:**

   ```bash
   cd development
   ./install.sh
   ```

4. **Verify the installation:**

   ```bash
   /founder-os:setup:verify
   ```

## How to Contribute

### Reporting Bugs

- Use the [Bug Report](https://github.com/thecloudtips/founder-workspace/issues/new?template=bug_report.yml) issue template
- Include your environment details (OS, Node version, Claude Code version)
- Include steps to reproduce the issue

### Suggesting Features

- Use the [Feature Request](https://github.com/thecloudtips/founder-workspace/issues/new?template=feature_request.yml) issue template
- Describe the problem you're trying to solve
- Explain your proposed solution

### Submitting Changes

1. Fork the repository
2. Create a feature branch: `git checkout -b feat/my-feature`
3. Make your changes following the existing code patterns
4. Test your changes
5. Commit with a descriptive message: `git commit -m "feat: add my feature"`
6. Push to your fork: `git push origin feat/my-feature`
7. Open a Pull Request

### PR Requirements

- Clear description of what changed and why
- Reference any related issues
- No breaking changes without discussion first
- Follow existing code style and patterns

## Code Style

- Follow the patterns established in existing commands, skills, and agents
- Commands go in `development/commands/<namespace>/<action>.md`
- Skills go in `development/skills/<namespace>/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`
- Agents go in `development/agents/<namespace>/`

## Code of Conduct

This project follows the [Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md). By participating, you are expected to uphold this code.

## Questions?

- Visit [fos.naluforge.com](https://fos.naluforge.com) for product documentation
- Read the [docs/](docs/) for technical documentation
- Open a [Discussion](https://github.com/thecloudtips/founder-workspace/discussions) for questions
```
